comment_text:
What an absolutely beautiful and emotional short documentary! 🦓✨
BBC Earth always has a special way of capturing the soul of wildlife, but this zebra journey feels particularly touching. The way the team follows her through danger, migration, and finally forming her own family is incredibly moving.

The cinematography is stunning — every frame feels like a painting, from the dusty plains to the gentle close-ups that reveal her courage and resilience. 🌍💛
It’s amazing how you can portray not just survival, but also hope, connection, and the quiet strength of an animal often overlooked.

Thank you for telling these powerful stories with respect, beauty, and heart. Truly inspiring work! 👏📽🌿
It’s a nice wildlife video. Greetings from Botswana 😊
It's a good thing the team's aircraft served a purpose for quick communication and a location isolating system.

Beautiful footage — it’s incredible to see the Nxai Pan zebra migration from the air. I filmed and photographed the same migration for my short video “Zebra Odyssey: Botswana’s Great Summer Migration,” capturing it from ground level. Seeing both perspectives really brings this journey to life.

This whole journey is insane, but the Socks storyline is chef's kiss. Losing the GPS, finding the faint signal, and then seeing that tiny newborn foal—I literally yelled, "YES!" What an absolute warrior. Do you think the new baby scent made her a bigger target right after the birth?

When nature writes its own endings, they’re always perfect. That tiny foal was worth every mile.

Wow, I didn’t expect to get emotional over zebras, but this story really got me. Hearing about Janet, Spirit, and Socks felt like following the journey of old friends through danger, loss, and survival. I can’t imagine how hard that migration must be — predators, exhaustion, and the constant uncertainty of who will make it. When the team finally picked up Socks’s signal, I actually felt relieved, like I was holding my breath for her the whole time. Nature is so raw and real, and moments like this remind me how strong these animals are. I really hope the rest of Janet’s herd is safe somewhere out there.
